explanation: "I <go> to school every day." — Used for movement to a place.
"Please <come> here." — Used for movement toward the speaker.
"I <eat> breakfast at 8 AM." — The act of consuming food.
"I <drink> water every morning." — The act of consuming liquids.
"I <sleep> eight hours every night." — To rest with eyes closed.
"I <walk> to the park after lunch." — To move on foot at a normal pace.
"She <runs> in the park every morning." — To move on foot quickly.
"I <read> a book before bed." — To look at and understand written words.
"He <writes> emails at work." — To put words on paper or a screen.
"She <speaks> three languages." — To use your voice to say words.
"<Listen> to the teacher, please." — To pay attention to sounds or words.
"I can <see> the mountains from here." — To perceive with your eyes.
"<Look> at this photo!" — To direct your eyes at something intentionally.
"We <watch> TV in the evening." — To look at something for a period of time.
"The children <play> in the garden." — To do an activity for fun.
"I <work> from Monday to Friday." — To do a job or task.
"She <studies> English at school." — To learn about a subject.
"I want to <learn> to cook." — To gain knowledge or a new skill.
"He <teaches> math to children." — To give knowledge to someone.
"Can you <help> me, please?" — To assist someone.
"Please <open> the window." — To make something not closed.
"<Close> the door, please." — To make something shut.
"Classes <start> at 9 AM." — To begin something.
"I <finish> work at 5 PM." — To complete or end something.
"I <buy> groceries on Saturdays." — To get something by paying money.
"They <sell> fresh fruit at the market." — To give something in exchange for money.
"I <give> her a present for her birthday." — To hand something to someone.
"<Take> your umbrella, it's raining." — To get or carry something.
"She <makes> dinner every evening." — To create or produce something.
"I <do> my homework after school." — To perform an action or task.
"I <have> two brothers." — To possess or own something.
"I <am> happy today." — To exist or describe a state.
"I <like> chocolate ice cream." — To enjoy something.
"I <love> my family." — To have a very strong positive feeling.
"I <want> a glass of water." — To desire or wish for something.
"I <need> a new notebook." — To require something.
"I <know> the answer." — To have information or be familiar with something.
"I <think> it's a good idea." — To use your mind to form opinions.
"I <feel> tired today." — To experience an emotion or physical sensation.
"I <live> in a small town." — To have your home in a place.
